qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: EXT opcode causes AttributeError crash bypassing all safety analysis
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory root cause is missing hook coverage for pickle.loads, _pickle.loads, and _pickle.load in hook.py run_hook(). This finding's root cause is UnsafeImportsML.analyze() in analysis.py crashing with AttributeError on EXT-opcode-generated ast.Import nodes. Different vulnerable files (hook.py vs analysis.py), different root-cause mechanisms (missing function hooks vs type-check omission on AST nodes), and different fix approaches (add entry points to hook list vs add isinstance guard before node.module access).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes run_hook() in hook.py missing hooks for pickle.loads, _pickle.loads, and _pickle.load. The ext_opcode_safety_bypass finding describes a different vulnerability in analysis.py where UnsafeImportsML.analyze() crashes on EXT-opcode-generated ast.Import nodes due to a missing type check. The finding describes a different root-cause mechanism in a different file from the advisory.
evidence: Advisory root cause: run_hook() in hook.py patches only pickle.load, pickle.Unpickler, _pickle.Unpickler — missing pickle.loads, _pickle.load, _pickle.loads
ext_opcode_safety_bypass root cause: UnsafeImportsML.analyze() in analysis.py:287 accesses node.module without isinstance check, crashes on ast.Import nodes from EXT opcodes (fickle.py:1404-1454)
Advisory fix: add missing entry points to hook list in hook.py (commit 8c24c6edabceab156cfd41f4d70b650e1cdad1f7)
ext_opcode_safety_bypass suggested fix: add isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom) guard before node.module access in analysis.py
